title: cheilanthes pteridioides subsp. guanchica
intro: Cheilanthes pteridioides subsp. guanchica is a type of fern native to the Canary Islands, characterized by its unique, lanceolate fronds. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as protecting against oxidative stress and promoting overall well-being. In herbal preparations, Cheilanthes pteridioides subsp. guanchica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use is not widely documented in modern medical literature.
